Andrew Chew of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff and opposing party Clayton Novotney.

RULING: SPECIALLY-APPEARING UNITED STATES FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT AGAINST DEFENDANT ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA
Parties stipulated to hear all matters simultaneously. Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Specially-Appearing United States Fire Insurance Company's Motion to Set Aside Default and Default Judgment Against Defendant Associated Insulation of California is denied. U.S. Fire lacks standing to bring this motion or appear in the action at this time. (W. Heritage Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (2011) 199 Cal. App. 4th 1196, 1205 n. 12 ["Pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 387, the trial court has discretion to permit a nonparty to intervene where the nonparty has a direct and immediate interest in the action"].) U.S. Fire has not demonstrated that it remains liable for any default judgment against Associated. (Id.) Moving party's request for Judicial Notice for Exhibits 2-11 is granted by the Court per Evidence Code 452(d). Court declines to take Judicial Notice of Exhibit 1. Order signed in chambers. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Miranda Rumsey, CSR # 14199, csrmirandar@gmail.com (Tel: 530-613-9821) (503/CML)

Off Calendar May-02-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Chris Hersom of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Clayton Novotney

RULING:
DEFENDANT IMO INDUSTRIES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Imo Industries Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff's negligence and strict liability causes of action are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to present evidence negating an essential element of plaintiff's claims. Even if the entirety of Mr. Salzmann's declaration is considered, it is not sufficient to sustain defendant's burden. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not now possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. By failing to demonstrate that plaintiff submitted factually devoid responses to contention discovery designed to elicit all facts and evidence plaintiff has to support his claims against defendant, defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Miles Lendway of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13684 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
